channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: After the initial ecstasy of freedom, released hostages face a long road to recovery
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When Ilana Gritzewsky returned to Israel after being held captive in Gaza for 55 days in November 2023, she had so much adrenaline coursing through her body she couldn’t sleep for two days. “You don’t understand that it’s really over," Gritzewsky recalled.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pioneering Black Army general who lived in El Paso dies at 100
item.description: Retired Army Maj. Gen. Edward Greer, who broke racial barriers during his long Army career, died at age 100 at his El Paso home on Wednesday, Jan. 29, his family said.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: A French journalist recounts 711 days of captivity as a hostage of Islamic extremists in Mali
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — For a foreign correspondent in Mali, the assignment seemed like a dream: as Islamic extremists upended the region, Olivier Dubois, a French journalist, secured a rare interview with a leader of JNIM, an al-Qaida’s affiliate in the Sahel. Or so he thought.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Midair collision kills 67 people in the deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: A midair collision between an Army helicopter and an American Airlines flight from Kansas has killed all 67 people aboard the two aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Tara Copp And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about the NTSB and the investigation into the DC plane cash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people brought renewed focus on the federal agency charged with investigating aviation disasters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mushers and dogs braved a horrific Alaska winter to deliver lifesaving serum 100 years ago
item.description: The Alaska Gold Rush town of Nome was hundreds of miles from anywhere, cut off by the frozen sea and under siege from a contagious disease known as the “strangling angel” for the way it suffocated children.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Midair collision kills 67 people in the deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — A midair collision between an Army helicopter and a jetliner killed all 67 people aboard the two aircraft, officials said Thursday, as they scrutinized the actions of the military pilot in the country's deadliest aviation disaster in almost a quarter century.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge extends court-monitoring agreement for children in Customs and Border Protection custody
item.description: McAllen, Texas (AP) — A federal judge extended a court agreement on Thursday ensuring safe and sanitary conditions for migrant children in federal custody a day after U.S. Customs and Border Protection was set to begin self-monitoring.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Honolulu police have arrested 6 people in connection with deadly New Year’s fireworks blast
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Police arrested a total of six people in connection with a deadly explosion of illegal fireworks in a Honolulu neighborhood on New Year’s Eve and confiscated 500 pounds (227 kilograms) of unused fireworks from the scene of the blast, authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gabbard battles senators over Snowden, Syria in fiery hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, President Trump’s pick to be the next director of national intelligence, endured a difficult hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Thursday as lawmakers pressed her on her views on Edward Snowden, spying, Syria and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: MMA fighter praises Hitler, calls him a 'good guy' in podcast
item.description: UFC fighter Bryce Mitchell praised Adolf Hitler and denied the Holocaust happened in the first episode of his new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KTLA 5

item.title: DC crash ‘almost identical’ to 1949 collision: Aviation expert
item.description: “There was a prior accident at Washington National 75 years ago almost identical to what happened last night.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, Fox 5

item.title: Mid-air collision draws attention to military helicopter training, previous accidents
item.description: To learn to fly a military helicopter is to take a master class in multitasking. You need both hands and both feet to control the aircraft while keeping a close eye on the horizon and your altitude.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee GOP passes school voucher expansion bill with backing from Trump
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tennessee lawmakers on Thursday approved legislation drastically expanding the number of families who can use taxpayer money on private schools regardless of income, a long-sought victory for Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aviation expert weighs in on how aircraft crash near DC may have happened
item.description: An aviation expert told Nexstar's WDCW that the altitude of both the passenger plane and Army helicopter may have played a principal role in the aircraft collision near Reagan National Airport (DCA).
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Marianne Faithfull, singer, actress and Rolling Stones muse, dies at 78
item.description: The 1960s icon passed in London surrounded by family, according to a statement from her record promotion group.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: NFL kicker Justin Tucker accused of sexual misconduct by 6 masseuses: report
item.description: Justin Tucker, kicker for the Baltimore Ravens, has been accused by six massage therapists at four separate businesses of sexually inappropriate behavior, the Baltimore Banner reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Dwyane Wade reveals cancer diagnosis, kidney surgery
item.description: The Chicago native shared the story of the "shocking" diagnosis and the emotional toll it took on him on the latest episode of his 'The Why' podcast.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles fire cleanup complicated by 'unprecedented' number of EVs with combustible lithium-ion batteries
item.description: California wildfire cleanup is complicated by highly combustible lithium-ion batteries found in electric and hybrid vehicles popular in Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missouri woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KRON4

item.title: First officer on deadly DC plane crash identified by father
item.description: RICHMOND HILL, Ga. (WSAV) — The family of one of the DC plane crash victims has been identified by his family. Nexstar affiliate WSAV confirmed through family that a Richmond Hill High School graduate was one of the 64 people who perished on the fatal American Airlines D.C. flight.
item.dc:creator: Dylan James, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DC plane crash timeline: Midair collision involves 67 passengers, crew members, soldiers
item.description: A timeline of the Washington, D.C., plane crash on Jan. 29 details the moments before and after an American Airlines passenger flight and Army helicopter collided over the Potomac.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing Colorado juvenile found in Houston; Suspect arrested by Bellaire police
item.description: BELLAIRE, Texas (KIAH) – A missing juvenile from Colorado has been safely recovered in Houston, thanks to the swift action by the Bellaire Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Estrada, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video captures fiery crash between American Airlines flight and Army Blackhawk
item.description: Editor's note: The video may appear graphic to some viewers. (NEXSTAR) - Images from the EarthCam video service captured images of what appears to be the fiery crash of an American Airlines jet over the Potomac River Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about the DC plane crash victims
item.description: Among those killed were figure skaters, union members, and a couple en route to visit their child at college.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Georgia councilwoman tells ex-BLM mayor to stop playing 'victim' after charging Africa vacay to city card
item.description: A councilwoman slammed South Fulton Mayor Khalid Kamau for chastising the "all-Black council" for criticizing his lavish expenses to the city credit card.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump holds briefing on plane crash near DC, suggests DEI programs contributed
item.description: President Donald Trump held a briefing Thursday morning on the deadly collision between an American Airlines plane and a military helicopter over the Potomac River.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen figure skaters and their coaches, group of hunters and students among victims of air crash
item.description: Passengers aboard the American Airlines flight that collided with an Army helicopter and crashed into the Potomac River included athletes returning from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Figure skaters among those on flight that collided with Army helicopter
item.description: U.S. Figure Skating released a statement saying athletes, coaches, and family members were on their way home from the National Development Camp held in conjunction with the U.S. Figure Skating Championship in Kansas when the collision happened.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, WGN 9

item.title: Black Hawk Army chopper crashes into American Airlines flight near DC and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Passenger jet with 64 aboard collides with Army helicopter while landing at Reagan Airport near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — An American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided Wednesday with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, prompting a large search-and-rescue operation in the nearby Potomac River.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump rescinds funding freeze but uncertainty lives on for wide array of environmental programs
item.description: A chaotic back-and-forth over President Donald Trump's freeze on federal grants and loans has farmers, activists, organizations and businesses wondering whether they will receive the funding they rely on.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gymnast dies after falling from pole while celebrating Philadelphia Eagles win
item.description: Tyler Sabapathy, 18, "sustained critical injuries" in the fall and passed away Tuesday, Temple University announced.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: Justice Department officials fired several prosecutors who worked on President Trump's criminal cases, saying they could not “trust” them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9
